overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, highly targeted academic and professional presence across specialized platforms. His content effectively engages a community of researchers, data scientists, and computational social scientists. Across LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Mastodon, he experiences high engagement rates particularly when sharing research milestones, tool releases like CooRnet v2.0, and career opportunities.

contentThemes.description: Research, tools, and methodologies focused on detecting coordinated link sharing and information operations.
Milestones including newly awarded research grants, conference presentations, and published papers.
Hiring opportunities for postdoctoral researchers and PhD positions in computational social science.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's high repost-to-like ratio by posting more interactive questions and open-ended research discussions to maximize peer amplification.
Utilize LinkedIn's larger audience for professional announcements like hiring and grant milestones, as these drive the highest absolute engagement volume.
Incorporate key hashtags and academic community tags on Mastodon to increase the visibility of technical software releases like CooRnet.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky operates as the most engaging platform by rate (6.14% average), showing excellent organic sharing and peer-to-peer amplification for new publications and student opportunities.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon shows stable, targeted engagement (4.55% average) centered on technical discussions and tool updates, ideal for niche academic dialog on aoir.social.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn yields the highest absolute volume of engagement, proving highly effective for professional announcements, recruitment, and high-level project funding milestones.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
